input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

<current_datetime>2026-04-04T00:55:39.790Z</current_datetime>

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
Here's how to make your page theme-aware:

## XAML — `AppThemeBinding`

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1C1C1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#FFFFFF}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#AAAAAA}" />

        <Button Text="Get Started"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#512BD4, Dark=#B39DDB}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

`AppThemeBinding` takes `Light` and `Dark` values and automatically switches when the OS theme changes — no event wiring needed.

## C# alternative — `SetAppTheme`

The equivalent in code-behind or C# markup:

```csharp
var page = new ContentPage();
page.SetAppTheme(ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Color.FromArgb("#FFFFFF"),   // light
    Color.FromArgb("#1C1C1E")); // dark

var title = new Label { Text = "Welcome", FontSize = 28 };
title.SetAppTheme(Label.TextColorProperty,
    Color.FromArgb("#000000"),
    Color.FromArgb("#FFFFFF"));

var subtitle = new Label { Text = "This page adapts to your system theme.", FontSize = 16 };
subtitle.SetAppTheme(Label.TextColorProperty,
    Color.FromArgb("#444444"),
    Color.FromArgb("#AAAAAA"));

var button = new Button { Text = "Get Started" };
button.SetAppTheme(Button.TextColorProperty,
    Color.FromArgb("#FFFFFF"),
    Color.FromArgb("#000000"));
button.SetAppTheme(Button.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Color.FromArgb("#512BD4"),
    Color.FromArgb("#B39DDB"));
```

`SetAppTheme(BindableProperty, lightValue, darkValue)` is the C# equivalent of `AppThemeBinding`. It reacts to OS theme changes at runtime the same way.

## Scaling up with resource dictionaries

For a real app, define colors in `App.xaml` and reference them by key:

```xml
<!-- App.xaml -->
<Application.Resources>
    <Color x:Key="PageBg">
        <!-- can't use AppThemeBinding on Color directly; use styles instead -->
    </Color>

    <Style TargetType="ContentPage">
        <Setter Property="BackgroundColor"
                Value="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1C1C1E}" />
    </Style>

    <Style TargetType="Label">
        <Setter Property="TextColor"
                Value="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#FFFFFF}" />
    </Style>
</Application.Resources>
```

This way every page and label inherits theming automatically, and you only maintain colors in one place.

**Key points:**
- `AppThemeBinding` responds live to OS theme changes — no manual subscription needed.
- It works on any `BindableProperty` (colors, images, strings, etc.).
- To detect the current theme in code: `Application.Current.RequestedTheme` returns `AppTheme.Light` or `AppTheme.Dark`.
- To force a specific theme: set `Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Dark`.
